2606.09424.title: Toward Signing Activity Projection in Sign Language Interaction
2606.09424.paper_summary: Social robots must interact robustly not only with users assumed by speech-centered systems but also with diverse users whose communication relies on different modalities, e.g., sign language. One important capability gap is predictive turn-taking with signing users. Although Voice Activity Projection (VAP) has been successfully used to model future voice activity in spoken interaction, it remains unclear whether the framework transfers to sign language interaction. This paper presents an initial transfer study of adapting a VAP architecture to dyadic sign language interaction. Using interaction recordings from the Public DGS Corpus, we derive binary signing activity streams from lexical sign annotations and formulate proxy tasks for turn-taking prediction. The model uses pose-derived hand, eye-region, and mouth-region features extracted for each signer. The results show that SHIFT/HOLD prediction is promising, especially with hand cues, while SHIFT-prediction remains difficult. These findings provide initial evidence for both the promise and the current limitations of transferring predictive turn-taking models from spoken interaction to sign language interaction. Predictive modeling of sign language interaction still requires sign-language-specific event definitions that go beyond speech-derived categories.
2606.09424.paper_summary_zh: 社交機器人必須不僅與以語音為中心的系統所假設的使用者進行穩健互動，還必須與依賴不同模式進行溝通的多樣化使用者互動，例如手語。 一個重要的能力差距是與手語使用者的預測輪流發言。 雖然語音活動預測（VAP）已成功用於建模口語互動中的未來語音活動，但尚不清楚該框架是否適用於手語互動。 本文呈現了一項初步的轉移研究，旨在將VAP架構適應於雙人手語互動。 我們使用公共DGS語料庫中的互動錄音，從詞彙手語註釋中推導出二元手語活動流，並為輪流發言預測制定代理任務。 該模型使用為每位手語者提取的姿勢衍生的手部、眼部區域和嘴部區域特徵。 結果顯示，SHIFT/HOLD預測是有前景的，特別是使用手部提示，而SHIFT預測仍然困難。 這些發現為將預測輪流發言模型從口語互動轉移到手語互動的潛力和當前限制提供了初步證據。 手語互動的預測建模仍然需要超越語音衍生類別的手語特定事件定義。
2606.09424.author: Takao Obi et.al.
2606.09424.authors: Takao Obi, Wang Yusong, Koji Inoue, Kotaro Funakoshi
